data.user_prompt: What is community-based mental health care, and who provides it in Louisville?
data.system_message: Answer as a local-search assistant for a person in Louisville, Kentucky. Prefer current local providers and cite source URLs when available.
data.tag: lmhg llm response perplexity What is community-based mental health care, and who provides it in Louisville?

sections.text:
**Community-based mental health care** is a service model that actively reduces barriers to treatment by delivering care directly in people’s homes, parks, community centers, and other familiar settings, rather than requiring individuals to visit a clinic [1]. It emphasizes **going to the person** who needs help, often using multidisciplinary teams (therapists, case managers) to provide hands-on support, family preservation, and crisis stabilization in the comfort of their own environment [1][2].

In **Louisville, Kentucky**, this care is provided by several key organizations:

| Provider | Type of Service | Key Details |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| **OMNI Louisville** | Community-Based Mental Health | Offers services in parks, homes, and community centers; runs the **FITT** (Family Intervention Treatment Team) and **Family Preservation Services** to prevent out-of-home placement [1]. |
| **Louisville Mental Health Group** | Community Support & Case Management | Provides **TCM** (Team Case Management) to meet clients where they are, helping with homelessness risks, food insecurity, and connecting to therapists [2]. |
| **Seven Counties Services** | Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic (CCBHC) | Kentucky’s leading **Community Mental Health Center**; offers 24/7 mobile crisis teams, outpatient treatment, and targeted case management regardless of insurance status [4][5]. |
| **Bridgehaven Mental Health Services** | Community-Based Care for Adults | Focuses on hope and recovery for adults living with mental illness through comprehensive community-based services [6]. |
| **UofL Health (Pain Hospital)** | Acute & Outpatient Services | Provides emergency psychiatric services, acute crisis stabilization, and adult outpatient therapy [7]. |

For immediate crisis support in Louisville, call **988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) or **988** for mobile crisis teams [4]. Seven Counties Services also offers a **24-hour crisis line** and telehealth options [4].

If you are seeking local providers, **OMNI Louisville** is located at **2906 Eastpoint Pkwy, Louisville, KY 40223**, and **Seven Counties Services** serves Jefferson County and surrounding areas [1][5].
